mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.3 million units signals a strengthening demand for residential construction that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.3M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production below 109.5 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces inventory levels and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 109.5 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 250K are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0 signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 2.0 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 19000 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 19000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production below 109.0 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces inventory levels and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 109.0 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 300,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 300K are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.4 million units and a 2-month increase in real GDP above 19050 signals a strengthening demand for residential construction and goods and services that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.4M and GDPC1 > 19050 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
